firearm.definition: A personal weapon that uses explosive powder to propel a projectile often made of lead.

fiscal.definition: A public official in certain countries having control of public revenue.

fit.definition: The degree to which something fits.

fleet.definition: A group of vessels or vehicles.

flesh.definition: The soft tissue of the body, especially muscle and fat.

flexibility.definition: The quality of being flexible; suppleness; pliability.

flourish.definition: A dramatic gesture such as the waving of a flag.

fluid.definition: Any substance which can flow with relative ease, tends to assume the shape of its container, and obeys Bernoulli's principle; a liquid, gas or plasma.

footage.definition: An amount of film or tape that has been used to record something.

foreigner.definition: A person from a foreign country.

forge.definition: Furnace or hearth where metals are heated prior to hammering them into shape.

formulate.definition: To reduce to, or express in, a formula; to put in a clear and definite form of statement or expression.

forth.definition: Forward in time, place or degree.

fragile.definition: Easily broken or destroyed, and thus often of subtle or intricate structure.

franchise.definition: The right to vote at a public election or referendum; see: suffrage, suffragette.

frankly.definition: (manner) In a frank, open or (too) honest manner.

freshman.definition: A novice; one in the rudiments of knowledge.

frustrated.definition: To disappoint or defeat; to vex by depriving of something expected or desired.

frustrating.definition: To disappoint or defeat; to vex by depriving of something expected or desired.

frustration.definition: The feeling of annoyance when one's actions are criticized or hindered

functional.definition: A function that takes a function as its argument; More precisely: A function y=f(x) whose argument x varies in a space of (real valued, complex valued) functions and whose value belongs to a monodimensional space. An example: the definite integration of integrable real functions in a real interval.

fundraising.definition: The process of collecting money by requesting donations from individuals and businesses.

funeral.definition: A ceremony to honour and remember a deceased person. Often distinguished from a memorial service by the presence of the body of the deceased.

gambling.definition: To take a risk, with the potential of a positive outcome.

gathering.definition: A meeting or get-together; a party or social function.

gaze.definition: A fixed look; a look of eagerness, wonder, or admiration; a continued look of attention.

gear.definition: Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.

generic.definition: A product sold under a generic name.

genocide.definition: The systematic killing of substantial numbers of people on the basis of their ethnicity, religion, political beliefs, social status, or other particularities.

gig.definition: A performing engagement by a musical group; or, generally, any job or role, especially for a musician or performer.

glimpse.definition: A brief look, glance, or peek.

glorious.definition: Exhibiting attributes, qualities, or acts that are worthy of or receive glory.

glory.definition: Great or overwhelming beauty or splendour.

governance.definition: The process, or the power, of governing; government or administration.

gravity.definition: The state or condition of having weight; weight; heaviness.

grid.definition: A rectangular array of squares or rectangles of equal size, such as in a crossword puzzle.

grin.definition: A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.

grind.definition: The act of reducing to powder, or of sharpening, by friction.

grip.definition: To take hold of, particularly with the hand.

guerrilla.definition: A soldier in a small independent group, fighting against the government or regular forces by surprise raids.

guidance.definition: The act or process of guiding.

gut.definition: The alimentary canal, especially the intestine.

H.definition: (chiefly Internet slang) Used as a filler response when one does not have a response to use. Almost always used by itself.

hail.definition: Balls or pieces of ice falling as precipitation, often in connection with a thunderstorm.

halfway.definition: Half of the way between two points; midway.

halt.definition: To limp; move with a limping gait.

handful.definition: The amount that a hand will grasp or contain.

handling.definition: A touching, controlling, managing, using, take care of, etc., with the hand or hands, or as with the hands.

harassment.definition: Persistent attacks and criticism causing worry and distress.

hardware.definition: Fixtures, equipment, tools and devices used for general-purpose construction and repair of a structure or object. Also such equipment as sold as stock by a store of the same name, e.g. hardware store.

harvest.definition: The third season of the year; autumn; fall.

haunt.definition: A place at which one is regularly found; a habitation or hangout.

hazard.definition: The chance of suffering harm; danger, peril, risk of loss.

heighten.definition: To make high; to raise higher; to elevate.

heritage.definition: An inheritance; property that may be inherited.

hierarchy.definition: A body of authoritative officials organized in nested ranks.

homeland.definition: The country that one regards as home.

hopeful.definition: Someone who is hoping for success or victory, especially as a candidate in a political election.

horizon.definition: The visible horizontal line or point (in all directions) that appears to connect the Earth to the sky.

horn.definition: A hard growth of keratin that protrudes from the top of the head of certain animals, usually paired.

hostage.definition: A person given as a pledge or security for the performance of the conditions of a treaty or similar agreement, such as to ensure the status of a vassal.

humanitarian.definition: A scholar of one of the subjects in the humanities.

humanity.definition: Mankind; human beings as a group.

humble.definition: (Baltimore) An arrest based on weak evidence intended to demean or punish the subject.

hydrogen.definition: The lightest chemical element (symbol H), with an atomic number of 1 and atomic weight of 1.00794.

I.definition: The name of the Latin-script letter I.

identification.definition: The act of identifying, or proving to be the same.

ideological.definition: Of or pertaining to an ideology.

ideology.definition: Doctrine, philosophy, body of beliefs or principles belonging to an individual or group.

idiot.definition: A person of low general intelligence.

ignorance.definition: The condition of being uninformed or uneducated. Lack of knowledge or information.

imagery.definition: The work of one who makes images or visible representation of objects.

imminent.definition: About to happen, occur, or take place very soon, especially of something which won't last long.

implementation.definition: The process of moving an idea from concept to reality. In business, engineering and other fields, implementation refers to the building process rather than the design process.

imprison.definition: To put in or as if in prison; confine.

inability.definition: Lack of the ability to do something; incapability.

inappropriate.definition: Not appropriate; not suitable for the situation, time, or place.

incarcerate.definition: To lock away; to imprison, especially for breaking the law.
